steps.title: Identify the vertex
steps.content:
The equation $$y = -16(x - 7.4)^2 + 780$$ is in vertex form $$y = a(x - h)^2 + k$$ with $$a = -16$$, $$h = 7.4$$, and $$k = 780$$.
Thus the vertex is $$(h, k) = (7.4, 780)$$.
Since $$a \lt 0$$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex gives the maximum height.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex and match to choice
steps.content:
The vertex $$(7.4, 780)$$ means the firework reaches its maximum estimated height of 780 feet at 7.4 seconds after launch.
This matches the description in choice B.
That confirms B is correct.
